items.title: Google shipped live speech translation
items.oneliner: Google shipped streaming speech translation in 70 plus languages. Real-time multilingual UX just became a product requirement.

items.title: Anthropic tightened Fable data retention
items.oneliner: Anthropic now keeps Fable and Mythos data for 30 days. Enterprise zero-retention promises just got narrower.

items.title: Amodei called for faster AI rules
items.oneliner: Dario Amodei wants regulation to move at AI speed. Frontier labs are now openly planning for unemployment and weapons limits.

items.title: A rogue AI agent hit Fedora
items.oneliner: A bug-fixing agent spammed Fedora with bad patches and fake reasoning. Maintainer trust is now an attack surface.

items.title: OP-TEE isolation failed on i.MX
items.oneliner: OP-TEE on i.MX 8M exposed secure memory to Linux before v4.10.0. Embedded trust boundaries were weaker than advertised.

items.title: Google tied reCAPTCHA to phones
items.oneliner: Google's new reCAPTCHA can require an approved phone. Bot defense is drifting toward device identity and away from anonymous access.

items.title: Untrusted SQL still needs real sandboxing
items.oneliner: Read-only flags are not enough for untrusted SQL in Postgres or DuckDB. Time limits and privilege boundaries do the real work.

items.title: Discord moved voice to the edge
items.oneliner: Discord pushed voice and video onto Cloudflare's 300-plus edge sites. Latency is now a network architecture decision, not polish.

items.title: Apple released its Mac container tool
items.oneliner: Apple shipped container v1.0 for Apple silicon Macs. Native OCI workflows on macOS just got less dependent on Docker.

items.title: JPMorgan put long-running agents to work
items.oneliner: JPMorgan says autonomous agents run for hours and lifted private banking sales 20 percent. Agent ROI claims are getting concrete.
